Article: How to Reset Your AcmeTech Account Password
Category: Account Access
Last Updated: March 2026

Overview
If you have forgotten your AcmeTech account password or are having trouble signing in, this article guides you through the steps to reset your password and regain access to your account. Password resets can typically be completed in under two minutes.

Self-Service Password Reset
To reset your password using the self-service flow, navigate to the AcmeTech login page and click the "Forgot password?" link below the password field. Enter the email address associated with your AcmeTech account and click "Send Reset Link." Check your inbox for an email from no-reply@acmetechsolutions.com. The email will arrive within two minutes in most cases; if you do not see it, please check your spam or junk folder. Click the reset link in the email. The link is valid for 30 minutes from the time it was sent. You will be directed to a page where you can create a new password. Your new password must be at least 12 characters long and must include at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character. Confirm your new password and click "Reset Password." You will be redirected to the login page and can sign in with your new password immediately.

Common Troubleshooting Issues
If you did not receive the reset email within five minutes, verify that you entered the correct email address. If your company uses a custom email domain, confirm that the AcmeTech notification email address is not blocked by your organization's email security filters. If you are part of an organization that uses Single Sign-On (SSO) for AcmeTech access, you cannot reset your AcmeTech password directly. Contact your IT administrator to reset your credentials through your corporate identity provider.

Account Lockout
If your account has been locked due to multiple failed login attempts, you will need to wait 15 minutes before attempting to sign in again or request a password reset as described above. Accounts locked due to a suspected security event may require manual verification by the AcmeTech Support team. Please submit a support ticket if your account remains locked after a successful password reset.

Contacting Support
If you continue to experience issues accessing your account after completing the password reset, please contact the AcmeTech Customer Support team by submitting a ticket through the support portal at support.acmetechsolutions.com or by emailing support@acmetechsolutions.com. Please include your account email address and a description of the issue you are experiencing.

Document ID: SUP-002
Category: Customer Support Knowledge Base
Title: Account Recovery Guide

Article: Recovering Access to a Locked or Compromised AcmeTech Account
Category: Account Access
Last Updated: February 2026

Overview
This article explains how to recover access to your AcmeTech account in scenarios beyond a standard password reset, including locked accounts, suspected unauthorized access, or situations where you no longer have access to your registered email address.

Recovering a Locked Account
If your account is locked due to multiple failed login attempts, the lockout period is 15 minutes for standard login attempt failures. You may use the self-service password reset during the lockout period to immediately regain access with a new password. If your account is locked due to a security review triggered by suspicious activity, the lockout may remain in place until cleared by AcmeTech's Support team. In this case, submit a support ticket with subject line "Locked Account – Security Review" and include your account email, organization name, and a brief description of your recent account activity.

Suspected Unauthorized Access
If you believe your AcmeTech account has been accessed without your authorization, take the following steps immediately. Sign in to your account if possible and navigate to Account Settings > Active Sessions to view all currently active sessions. Revoke any sessions you do not recognize using the "Sign out" option next to each session. Change your password immediately using the settings available in Account Security. If you cannot sign in, use the self-service password reset. Enable multi-factor authentication if it is not already enabled. Contact AcmeTech Support to report the suspected breach. Our security team will investigate the account activity log and assist with containment.

Changing Your Registered Email Address
If you need to update the email address associated with your account, sign in and navigate to Account Settings > Profile. Enter your new email address and confirm by re-entering your current password. A verification email will be sent to the new address. Click the verification link to confirm the change. If you no longer have access to your original email address, contact Support with identity verification documentation before the change can be processed.

Organization Administrator Recovery
If you are an organization administrator who has lost access to your account and cannot use self-service recovery, AcmeTech requires multi-factor identity verification before restoring access. Contact Support via phone through your dedicated account number or email support with a formal written request on company letterhead. Processing time for administrator account recovery requests is one to two business days.

Document ID: SUP-004

Title: Billing Issues Troubleshooting Guide

Article: Resolving Billing Issues with Your AcmeTech Account
Category: Billing and Subscriptions
Last Updated: March 2026

Overview
This article addresses common billing issues that AcmeTech customers may encounter, including failed payment processing, unexpected charges, invoice discrepancies, and subscription cancellations. For urgent billing issues that are preventing access to your account, contact the AcmeTech Billing Support team immediately.

Failed Payment Processing
If a payment for your AcmeTech subscription has failed, you will receive an email notification from billing@acmetechsolutions.com. Your account will remain active for seven days following a failed payment to allow time to resolve the issue. To resolve a failed payment, sign in and navigate to Billing > Payment Methods. Verify that your payment method details are current, including the card number, expiration date, and billing address. Update the payment method if necessary and click "Retry Payment." If the payment continues to fail, contact your card issuer or bank to confirm that there are no restrictions on transactions to AcmeTech.

Unexpected Charges
If you see a charge from AcmeTech that you do not recognize, check your account billing history in the Billing section of Account Settings for a detailed breakdown of all charges. Common reasons for unexpected charges include mid-cycle plan upgrades, additional user seat purchases, and overage charges for usage-based features. If you are unable to identify the source of a charge after reviewing your billing history, contact the AcmeTech Billing team with the charge date and amount and a team member will investigate within two business days.

Invoice Discrepancies
If your invoice does not match your expected subscription pricing, verify that your plan tier and any discounts or promotional pricing are reflected correctly in your Account Settings. Invoices can be downloaded as PDFs from the Billing History section. If the discrepancy persists, submit a support ticket with the invoice number and a description of the expected versus actual amounts.

Requesting a Refund
AcmeTech's standard policy is that subscription fees are non-refundable except in cases of verified billing errors or circumstances covered by AcmeTech's 30-day satisfaction guarantee for new subscriptions. Refund requests must be submitted within 30 days of the charge date. Contact the Billing team with your account information and the reason for the refund request.

Account Suspension
If your account has been suspended due to non-payment, access to the AcmeTech platform will be restricted until the overdue balance is paid. Contact the Billing team to arrange payment and request reactivation. Account data is retained for 90 days following suspension before it is subject to deletion.

Document ID: SUP-008
Category: Customer Support Knowledge Base
Title: Mobile App Troubleshooting Guide

Article: Troubleshooting AcmeTech Mobile App Issues
Category: Mobile App
Last Updated: March 2026

Overview
This article covers the most common issues experienced by users of the AcmeTech mobile app on iOS and Android devices, along with step-by-step troubleshooting instructions. If your issue is not covered here, please submit a support ticket through the app or the support portal.

Cannot Sign In to the Mobile App
If you are unable to sign in to the AcmeTech mobile app, try the following steps. Confirm you are using the correct email address and password associated with your AcmeTech account. If you have forgotten your password, use the "Forgot password?" link on the login screen. Verify that your device has an active internet connection by opening another app that requires connectivity. If your organization uses Single Sign-On, tap "Sign in with SSO" and enter your organization's SSO domain rather than your password. Ensure your device's operating system is up to date; the AcmeTech app requires iOS 16 or higher and Android 12 or higher. If the issue persists, uninstall and reinstall the app from the App Store or Google Play.

App Crashes or Freezes
If the AcmeTech mobile app crashes or becomes unresponsive, force-close the app and reopen it. If the app crashes consistently on launch, uninstall it and reinstall the latest version from the app store. Check that your device has at least 200 MB of available storage. Clearing the app's cache on Android devices (Settings > Apps > AcmeTech > Storage > Clear Cache) can resolve many performance-related issues. If crashes occur in a specific feature or after a recent app update, submit a support ticket through the support portal and include your device model, OS version, and a description of the steps that reproduce the crash.

Notifications Not Arriving
If you are not receiving push notifications from the AcmeTech mobile app, check that notification permissions are enabled for AcmeTech in your device settings. On iOS, navigate to Settings > AcmeTech > Notifications and ensure "Allow Notifications" is enabled. On Android, navigate to Settings > Apps > AcmeTech > Notifications. If notifications are enabled at the device level, check in-app notification preferences by navigating to Profile > Notification Settings within the AcmeTech app.

Data Not Syncing
If data in the mobile app appears out of date, pull down on the main screen to force a manual refresh. Check your device's network connectivity. If you are connected to Wi-Fi, try switching to your mobile data connection to rule out a Wi-Fi-specific issue. Sign out and sign back in to the app to force a full data refresh. If a specific record or document is not syncing, check whether the same data is accessible via the web platform to determine whether the issue is app-specific or account-specific.

items.question: How do I configure Single Sign-On using SAML 2.0 in AcmeTech?
items.answer:
To configure Single Sign-On (SSO) using SAML 2.0 in AcmeTech:

1. Navigate to **Account Settings > Security > Single Sign-On** in AcmeTech.
2. Select **SAML 2.0** as the SSO type.
3. Download the **AcmeTech Service Provider metadata** from the configuration page.
4. Upload the metadata to your identity provider to create the AcmeTech application.
5. In AcmeTech, enter your identity provider's metadata URL or upload the metadata XML file.
6. Configure the attribute mapping to specify which identity provider attributes correspond to AcmeTech's required user fields: **email address, first name, and last name**.
7. Save the configuration and click **"Test SSO Connection"** to validate before enabling.
8. Once tested, activate SSO for your organization by toggling **"Enforce SSO Login"**.

Document ID: SUP-009
Category: Customer Support Knowledge Base
Title: SSO Setup Guide

Article: Setting Up Single Sign-On for Your AcmeTech Organization
Category: Security and Integrations
Last Updated: February 2026

Overview
AcmeTech supports Single Sign-On (SSO) integration with corporate identity providers via SAML 2.0 and OpenID Connect (OIDC). Enabling SSO allows your users to sign in to AcmeTech using their existing corporate credentials, eliminating the need for separate AcmeTech passwords and enabling centralized access management. SSO setup is available on Professional and Enterprise plans.

Prerequisites
Before configuring SSO, ensure you have administrator access to both your corporate identity provider and your AcmeTech organization account. You will need your identity provider's metadata URL or XML file (for SAML) or your identity provider's discovery endpoint URL, client ID, and client secret (for OIDC). SSO configuration should be tested in a non-production environment before being activated for all users.

Configuring SAML 2.0 SSO
Navigate to Account Settings > Security > Single Sign-On in AcmeTech. Select SAML 2.0 as the SSO type. Download the AcmeTech Service Provider metadata from the configuration page and upload it to your identity provider to create the AcmeTech application. In AcmeTech, enter your identity provider's metadata URL or upload the metadata XML file. Configure the attribute mapping to specify which identity provider attributes correspond to AcmeTech's required user fields: email address, first name, and last name. Save the configuration and click "Test SSO Connection" to validate before enabling.

Configuring OIDC SSO
Navigate to Account Settings > Security > Single Sign-On and select OpenID Connect as the SSO type. Enter your identity provider's discovery endpoint URL, client ID, and client secret. Configure the required scopes: openid, email, and profile. Enter the redirect URI displayed on the AcmeTech configuration page into your identity provider's application settings. Save the configuration and test the SSO flow using the "Test SSO Connection" button.

Activating SSO for All Users
Once the SSO configuration is tested and confirmed working, activate it for your organization by toggling "Enforce SSO Login" in the SSO configuration settings. After SSO is enforced, users will be redirected to your identity provider's login page when they attempt to sign in to AcmeTech. Existing AcmeTech-native passwords will no longer be accepted. Ensure all users have active accounts in your identity provider before enforcing SSO to prevent lockouts.

Troubleshooting SSO Issues
Common SSO configuration errors include certificate mismatch between the identity provider and AcmeTech's expected signature validation settings, incorrect attribute mapping causing user provisioning failures, and redirect URI mismatches in OIDC configurations. If users are unable to complete the SSO login flow, check the error details displayed in the browser and compare them against the SSO troubleshooting guide in the AcmeTech developer documentation.

Document ID: ENG-007
Category: Engineering Documentation
Title: Authentication Architecture

AcmeTech Solutions Authentication Architecture
Version: 2.0
Last Updated: January 2026
Owner: Security Engineering Team

Overview
This document describes the authentication architecture for AcmeTech's platform, covering both customer-facing authentication and internal service-to-service authentication. A robust, layered authentication architecture is foundational to the security of AcmeTech's systems and customer data.

Customer Authentication
AcmeTech customers authenticate to the platform using OAuth 2.0 with the Authorization Code Flow with PKCE for web and mobile applications. Upon successful authentication, the identity provider issues a short-lived JSON Web Token (JWT) with a 15-minute expiration and a refresh token with a 30-day expiration. JWTs are signed using RS256 and validated by the API Gateway before any request is forwarded to backend services. Token rotation is enforced on every refresh token use.

Identity Provider
AcmeTech operates its own identity provider built on the open-source Keycloak platform, customized for our multi-tenant architecture. Each customer organization is provisioned as a separate Keycloak realm, enabling complete logical isolation of authentication contexts. The identity provider supports username/password authentication, SSO via SAML 2.0 and OIDC federation with corporate identity providers, and TOTP-based multi-factor authentication.

Multi-Factor Authentication
MFA is required for all AcmeTech administrative console users and is strongly recommended for all end users. MFA enforcement at the customer organization level can be configured by the organization's administrator. AcmeTech supports TOTP-based authenticators and WebAuthn hardware security keys. SMS-based MFA is not supported due to known vulnerabilities associated with SIM swapping attacks.

Service-to-Service Authentication
Internal microservice-to-microservice communication is authenticated using mutual TLS (mTLS). Each service is issued a short-lived X.509 certificate by the cluster certificate authority. Certificate rotation occurs every 24 hours and is managed automatically by the service mesh. Services validate peer certificates against the trusted CA bundle at connection establishment. Service accounts in Kubernetes are issued projected service account tokens with audience-restricted claims for additional authorization enforcement.

Session Management
Customer sessions are managed through the identity provider with activity-based session timeout after 60 minutes of inactivity and an absolute maximum session duration of 12 hours. Session revocation is propagated to the API Gateway token validation cache within 30 seconds of logout or administrative revocation. The authentication audit log captures all authentication events, including successful logins, failed attempts, MFA challenges, and session revocations.

Document ID: SUP-007
Category: Customer Support Knowledge Base
Title: Security Settings Guide

Article: Configuring Security Settings for Your AcmeTech Organization
Category: Security and Compliance
Last Updated: April 2026

Overview
AcmeTech provides a comprehensive set of security settings that organization administrators can configure to meet their organization's security and compliance requirements. This article covers multi-factor authentication enforcement, session management, IP allowlisting, and audit log access.

Enabling Organization-Wide Multi-Factor Authentication
Organization administrators can enforce MFA for all users in the organization by navigating to Account Settings > Security > Authentication. Toggle the "Require Multi-Factor Authentication" setting to enabled. Once enabled, all users who have not already configured MFA will be required to set up an authenticator app at their next login before they can access the platform. Users who have already configured MFA are unaffected. AcmeTech supports TOTP-based authenticator apps and WebAuthn hardware security keys for MFA.

Session Management Settings
Administrators can configure the session timeout duration and the maximum session length for their organization. Navigate to Account Settings > Security > Sessions. The session inactivity timeout can be set between 15 minutes and 24 hours. The absolute maximum session duration can be set between 1 hour and 30 days. Sessions that exceed either threshold are automatically terminated, requiring users to sign in again. Administrators can view and revoke active sessions for any user in their organization from the User Management section.

IP Allowlisting
Enterprise plan customers can restrict AcmeTech access to specific IP address ranges by configuring an IP allowlist. Navigate to Account Settings > Security > IP Allowlist and enter the authorized IP address ranges in CIDR notation. When an allowlist is active, access attempts from IP addresses outside the configured ranges will be blocked with an access denied error. Test your allowlist configuration carefully before enabling it to ensure that all legitimate users are included. The allowlist can be disabled immediately if users are inadvertently locked out.

Audit Log Access
AcmeTech maintains a comprehensive audit log of all administrative actions, user login events, permission changes, and data access events within your organization. Administrators can access the audit log by navigating to Account Settings > Security > Audit Log. Logs can be filtered by date range, event type, and user. Audit logs can be exported in CSV or JSON format for integration with your SIEM or compliance tooling. Log retention is 12 months for Professional plan customers and 24 months for Enterprise plan customers.

Security Incident Reporting
If you suspect a security incident involving your AcmeTech organization, contact the AcmeTech Security team immediately at security@acmetechsolutions.com. Include your organization name, a description of the suspected incident, and any relevant details. AcmeTech's security team will acknowledge your report within four hours and provide a status update within 24 hours.

items.question: If a customer's account is locked, how does the process differ between a standard failed-login lockout and a lockout triggered by a security review?
items.answer:
For a **standard failed-login lockout**, the account is automatically unlocked after **15 minutes**, and the customer can use the self-service password reset to regain access immediately.

The customer must submit a support ticket with the subject line **"Locked Account – Security Review"**, including their account email, organization name, and a brief description of recent activity.

Document ID: SUP-002
Category: Customer Support Knowledge Base
Title: Account Recovery Guide

Article: Recovering Access to a Locked or Compromised AcmeTech Account
Category: Account Access
Last Updated: February 2026

Overview
This article explains how to recover access to your AcmeTech account in scenarios beyond a standard password reset, including locked accounts, suspected unauthorized access, or situations where you no longer have access to your registered email address.

Recovering a Locked Account
If your account is locked due to multiple failed login attempts, the lockout period is 15 minutes for standard login attempt failures. You may use the self-service password reset during the lockout period to immediately regain access with a new password. If your account is locked due to a security review triggered by suspicious activity, the lockout may remain in place until cleared by AcmeTech's Support team. In this case, submit a support ticket with subject line "Locked Account – Security Review" and include your account email, organization name, and a brief description of your recent account activity.

Suspected Unauthorized Access
If you believe your AcmeTech account has been accessed without your authorization, take the following steps immediately. Sign in to your account if possible and navigate to Account Settings > Active Sessions to view all currently active sessions. Revoke any sessions you do not recognize using the "Sign out" option next to each session. Change your password immediately using the settings available in Account Security. If you cannot sign in, use the self-service password reset. Enable multi-factor authentication if it is not already enabled. Contact AcmeTech Support to report the suspected breach. Our security team will investigate the account activity log and assist with containment.

Changing Your Registered Email Address
If you need to update the email address associated with your account, sign in and navigate to Account Settings > Profile. Enter your new email address and confirm by re-entering your current password. A verification email will be sent to the new address. Click the verification link to confirm the change. If you no longer have access to your original email address, contact Support with identity verification documentation before the change can be processed.

Organization Administrator Recovery
If you are an organization administrator who has lost access to your account and cannot use self-service recovery, AcmeTech requires multi-factor identity verification before restoring access. Contact Support via phone through your dedicated account number or email support with a formal written request on company letterhead. Processing time for administrator account recovery requests is one to two business days.

Document ID: SUP-007
Category: Customer Support Knowledge Base
Title: Security Settings Guide

Article: Configuring Security Settings for Your AcmeTech Organization
Category: Security and Compliance
Last Updated: April 2026

Overview
AcmeTech provides a comprehensive set of security settings that organization administrators can configure to meet their organization's security and compliance requirements. This article covers multi-factor authentication enforcement, session management, IP allowlisting, and audit log access.

Enabling Organization-Wide Multi-Factor Authentication
Organization administrators can enforce MFA for all users in the organization by navigating to Account Settings > Security > Authentication. Toggle the "Require Multi-Factor Authentication" setting to enabled. Once enabled, all users who have not already configured MFA will be required to set up an authenticator app at their next login before they can access the platform. Users who have already configured MFA are unaffected. AcmeTech supports TOTP-based authenticator apps and WebAuthn hardware security keys for MFA.

Session Management Settings
Administrators can configure the session timeout duration and the maximum session length for their organization. Navigate to Account Settings > Security > Sessions. The session inactivity timeout can be set between 15 minutes and 24 hours. The absolute maximum session duration can be set between 1 hour and 30 days. Sessions that exceed either threshold are automatically terminated, requiring users to sign in again. Administrators can view and revoke active sessions for any user in their organization from the User Management section.

IP Allowlisting
Enterprise plan customers can restrict AcmeTech access to specific IP address ranges by configuring an IP allowlist. Navigate to Account Settings > Security > IP Allowlist and enter the authorized IP address ranges in CIDR notation. When an allowlist is active, access attempts from IP addresses outside the configured ranges will be blocked with an access denied error. Test your allowlist configuration carefully before enabling it to ensure that all legitimate users are included. The allowlist can be disabled immediately if users are inadvertently locked out.

Audit Log Access
AcmeTech maintains a comprehensive audit log of all administrative actions, user login events, permission changes, and data access events within your organization. Administrators can access the audit log by navigating to Account Settings > Security > Audit Log. Logs can be filtered by date range, event type, and user. Audit logs can be exported in CSV or JSON format for integration with your SIEM or compliance tooling. Log retention is 12 months for Professional plan customers and 24 months for Enterprise plan customers.

Security Incident Reporting
If you suspect a security incident involving your AcmeTech organization, contact the AcmeTech Security team immediately at security@acmetechsolutions.com. Include your organization name, a description of the suspected incident, and any relevant details. AcmeTech's security team will acknowledge your report within four hours and provide a status update within 24 hours.

Document ID: PM-005
Category: Product Management
Title: User Research Findings Report

AcmeTech Solutions User Research Findings Report
Q1 2026 Enterprise Customer Study
Owner: Olivia Grant, Product Manager – User Research

Executive Summary
This report presents findings from AcmeTech's Q1 2026 enterprise customer research study. The study was conducted to understand the primary pain points experienced by enterprise customers in their first 90 days on the AcmeTech platform, their expectations for advanced administrative controls, and their willingness to adopt AI-powered features. Twenty-four in-depth interviews were conducted with users and administrators across eight enterprise customer organizations.

Research Methodology
Interviews were conducted via video call and lasted approximately 45 minutes each. Participants were recruited through the Customer Success team and represented a cross-section of roles including administrators, power users, and occasional users. Interviews were recorded with participant consent and analyzed using thematic analysis. Quantitative validation of qualitative themes was conducted through a follow-up survey distributed to 180 enterprise customers.

Key Finding 1: Onboarding Complexity is the Primary Retention Risk
67% of interviewed users reported feeling overwhelmed during their first two weeks on the platform. The most frequently cited pain point was the lack of role-specific onboarding flows; current onboarding presents all features regardless of the user's role, creating cognitive overload. Users who received a dedicated onboarding session with a Customer Success Manager reported significantly higher confidence and feature adoption at 90 days. Recommendation: Invest in role-based onboarding tracks and evaluate automation of the initial CSM touchpoint through guided in-product onboarding.

Key Finding 2: Administrators Require Stronger Access Controls
91% of interviewed administrators indicated that the current role-based access control model is insufficient for their organization's security and compliance requirements. Administrators specifically requested field-level permissions and the ability to restrict user access to specific customer segments within the platform. This finding directly supports the Enterprise Readiness roadmap theme and is aligned with feedback tracked through the product request intake form over the past two quarters.

Key Finding 3: AI Feature Adoption Requires Trust Building
While enthusiasm for AI-powered features is high, with 78% of participants expressing interest in the AI document summarization concept, adoption willingness is contingent on transparency and control. Participants expressed concern about AI-generated content being surfaced without clear attribution or the ability to review the source material. Recommendation: Design AI features with explicit source citation, confidence indicators, and the ability for users to inspect the data driving AI outputs.

Key Finding 4: Collaboration Features Drive Team Adoption
Teams that use AcmeTech's collaboration features, including shared workspaces and comment threads, had a 40% higher breadth of adoption score at 90 days than teams that used the platform primarily as an individual tool. Recommendation: Prioritize collaboration feature visibility in the onboarding flow and add social prompts that encourage users to invite colleagues to engage with their work.

Article: How to Reset Your AcmeTech Account Password
Category: Account Access
Last Updated: March 2026

Overview
If you have forgotten your AcmeTech account password or are having trouble signing in, this article guides you through the steps to reset your password and regain access to your account. Password resets can typically be completed in under two minutes.

Self-Service Password Reset
To reset your password using the self-service flow, navigate to the AcmeTech login page and click the "Forgot password?" link below the password field. Enter the email address associated with your AcmeTech account and click "Send Reset Link." Check your inbox for an email from no-reply@acmetechsolutions.com. The email will arrive within two minutes in most cases; if you do not see it, please check your spam or junk folder. Click the reset link in the email. The link is valid for 30 minutes from the time it was sent. You will be directed to a page where you can create a new password. Your new password must be at least 12 characters long and must include at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character. Confirm your new password and click "Reset Password." You will be redirected to the login page and can sign in with your new password immediately.

Common Troubleshooting Issues
If you did not receive the reset email within five minutes, verify that you entered the correct email address. If your company uses a custom email domain, confirm that the AcmeTech notification email address is not blocked by your organization's email security filters. If you are part of an organization that uses Single Sign-On (SSO) for AcmeTech access, you cannot reset your AcmeTech password directly. Contact your IT administrator to reset your credentials through your corporate identity provider.

Account Lockout
If your account has been locked due to multiple failed login attempts, you will need to wait 15 minutes before attempting to sign in again or request a password reset as described above. Accounts locked due to a suspected security event may require manual verification by the AcmeTech Support team. Please submit a support ticket if your account remains locked after a successful password reset.

Contacting Support
If you continue to experience issues accessing your account after completing the password reset, please contact the AcmeTech Customer Support team by submitting a ticket through the support portal at support.acmetechsolutions.com or by emailing support@acmetechsolutions.com. Please include your account email address and a description of the issue you are experiencing.

items.question: What is the default API rate limit for a Professional plan customer, and what happens when the limit is exceeded?
items.answer:
The default API rate limit for a **Professional Plan** customer is **1,000 requests per minute**.

When the limit is exceeded, the AcmeTech API returns an **HTTP 429 Too Many Requests** response, including a **Retry-After** header indicating the number of seconds to wait before retrying. You should implement **exponential backoff with jitter** when encountering this error.

**Sources:**
- SUP-005

Category: Customer Support Knowledge Base
Title: API Rate Limits Guide

Article: Understanding AcmeTech API Rate Limits
Category: API and Integrations
Last Updated: March 2026

Overview
AcmeTech enforces API rate limits to ensure fair usage of the platform and to protect system stability for all customers. This article explains how rate limits work, how to check your current usage, how to handle rate limit errors, and how to request a rate limit increase.

Default Rate Limits
AcmeTech API rate limits are applied per customer organization. The default rate limits by plan tier are: Starter Plan: 500 requests per minute. Professional Plan: 1,000 requests per minute. Enterprise Plan: Customizable, with a default of 5,000 requests per minute configurable up to your contracted maximum. Rate limits apply to all API endpoints collectively. Specific high-throughput endpoints such as batch operations may have additional per-endpoint limits documented in the API reference.

How Rate Limiting Works
AcmeTech uses a sliding window rate limiting algorithm. Your request quota is replenished continuously rather than resetting at a fixed interval. This means that if you send 500 requests in the first 10 seconds of a minute, you are not allowed to send 500 more requests at the start of the next minute; instead, you regain capacity gradually as older requests move out of the sliding window.

Handling Rate Limit Errors
When your request rate exceeds the configured limit, the AcmeTech API returns an HTTP 429 Too Many Requests response. The response includes a Retry-After header indicating the number of seconds you should wait before retrying. Your integration should implement exponential backoff with jitter when encountering 429 responses. Do not immediately retry at full request volume after the Retry-After period; increase the delay progressively if rate limit errors persist. Monitoring your API usage against your rate limits proactively is the best way to avoid unexpected 429 errors in production.

Monitoring API Usage
Organization administrators can view real-time and historical API usage in the AcmeTech developer dashboard under Settings > API Usage. Usage data is updated every minute. Alerts can be configured to notify your team when API usage exceeds a specified percentage of your rate limit. Setting up usage alerts is strongly recommended for all integrations with high request volumes.

Requesting a Rate Limit Increase
Enterprise customers who require higher rate limits than their default allocation can request an increase through their dedicated account manager or by submitting a support ticket with the subject "Rate Limit Increase Request." Include your organization name, current limit, requested limit, and a brief description of your use case. Rate limit increases for Enterprise customers are typically approved within two business days.

Category: Customer Support Knowledge Base
Title: Subscription Upgrade Guide

Article: How to Upgrade Your AcmeTech Subscription Plan
Category: Billing and Subscriptions
Last Updated: April 2026

Overview
This article explains how to upgrade your AcmeTech subscription to a higher plan tier, what to expect during the upgrade process, and how billing is handled for mid-cycle upgrades.

Available Subscription Plans
AcmeTech offers three subscription tiers. The Starter plan is designed for small teams and includes core platform access for up to 10 users with standard support. The Professional plan is designed for growing teams and includes advanced features, up to 50 users, and priority support. The Enterprise plan is designed for large organizations and includes full feature access, unlimited users, custom integrations, dedicated account management, and enterprise SLA support.

How to Upgrade
To upgrade your subscription, sign in to your AcmeTech account as an organization administrator and navigate to the Billing section in your Account Settings. Click "Change Plan" and select the plan you wish to upgrade to. Review the plan features and pricing summary on the plan comparison page. Click "Confirm Upgrade" to proceed. If your account is on a monthly billing cycle, the new plan pricing takes effect immediately and your first invoice at the new rate will be generated at the next billing date. If your account is on an annual billing cycle, you will be charged a prorated amount for the remainder of the current annual term at the time of the upgrade. Your upgraded plan features will be available immediately upon confirmation.

User Seat Adjustments
When upgrading to a plan with a higher user limit, additional user seats become available immediately. If your current user count exceeds the limit of your new plan, you will be prompted to remove users before the upgrade can be confirmed. AcmeTech does not automatically remove users; an administrator must manage user access through the User Management section.

Enterprise Plan Upgrades
Upgrades to the Enterprise plan cannot be completed through the self-service billing portal. Contact your AcmeTech account manager or reach out to the sales team at sales@acmetechsolutions.com to discuss your Enterprise plan requirements. The Enterprise onboarding process typically takes three to five business days and includes a kickoff call with your dedicated account team.

Billing Inquiries
For questions about subscription pricing, invoice details, or billing adjustments, contact the AcmeTech Billing team via the support portal at support.acmetechsolutions.com or call the billing hotline available on your account contact page.

Category: HR Policies
Title: Health Insurance Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Employee Health Insurance Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: People Operations and Benefits Administration

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions provides comprehensive health insurance coverage to all eligible full-time employees as part of its total compensation package. This document outlines the insurance plans available, employee contribution requirements, enrollment procedures, and benefit coverage details.

Eligible Employees
All full-time employees working 30 or more hours per week are eligible for health insurance coverage beginning on the first day of the month following their hire date. Employees may also enroll eligible dependents, including legal spouses, domestic partners, and dependent children up to the age of 26.

Available Plans
AcmeTech offers three plan tiers through its insurance carrier partnership: the Standard Plan, the Enhanced Plan, and the Premium Plan. The Standard Plan covers 80% of in-network medical costs after a $1,500 annual deductible. The Enhanced Plan covers 90% of in-network medical costs after a $750 deductible and includes dental and vision coverage. The Premium Plan covers 100% of in-network medical costs after a $250 deductible and includes enhanced dental, vision, and mental health benefits.

Employee Contributions
AcmeTech contributes 80% of the monthly premium for the Standard Plan for all eligible employees. Employee contributions for the Enhanced and Premium Plans vary based on coverage tier and are deducted from bi-weekly paychecks on a pre-tax basis. Dependent coverage is available at an additional cost to the employee.

Enrollment and Changes
Open enrollment takes place each year during the month of October, with coverage changes effective January 1. Outside of open enrollment, employees may make changes to their coverage within 30 days of a qualifying life event, including marriage, divorce, birth or adoption of a child, or loss of coverage under another plan. Enrollment changes must be submitted through the HR benefits portal.

Mental Health and Wellness Benefits
All health insurance plans include coverage for mental health services, including therapy, psychiatric consultations, and substance use treatment. AcmeTech also provides all employees with access to the HealthMind employee assistance program, which offers confidential counseling services, financial wellness support, and well-being resources at no cost.

Document ID: FIN-011
Category: Finance & Tax
Title: Revenue Recognition Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Revenue Recognition Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: Finance

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions recognizes revenue in accordance with ASC 606, Revenue from Contracts with Customers. This policy ensures that revenue is recorded accurately, consistently, and in compliance with applicable accounting standards. All revenue-related transactions must be reviewed by Finance before recognition.

Five-Step Model
AcmeTech applies the ASC 606 five-step model to all customer contracts. Step 1: Identify the contract with the customer. Step 2: Identify the performance obligations in the contract. Step 3: Determine the transaction price. Step 4: Allocate the transaction price to each performance obligation. Step 5: Recognize revenue when or as each performance obligation is satisfied.

SaaS Subscription Revenue
Subscription revenue is recognized ratably over the contract term, beginning on the date the customer's access to the platform is provisioned. Annual and multi-year subscription payments received in advance are recorded as deferred revenue and recognized proportionally on a monthly basis. Any discounts or variable consideration in subscription contracts must be evaluated and documented by Finance before contract execution.

Professional Services Revenue
Revenue from implementation and consulting services is recognized as services are delivered, based on the percentage of completion method. Project milestones used for revenue recognition must be documented in the statement of work and approved by Finance prior to contract signing. Revenue from time-and-materials engagements is recognized monthly based on actual hours delivered.

Contract Modifications
Any changes to existing customer contracts that affect the scope, price, or term must be reviewed by Finance before execution. Modifications may require revenue to be reallocated among performance obligations or may be treated as a separate contract depending on the nature of the change. Sales and Legal must engage Finance on all material contract modifications.

Document ID: PM-010
Category: Product Management
Title: Stakeholder Communication Plan

AcmeTech Solutions Product Stakeholder Communication Plan
FY2026 Q3 Product Updates
Owner: Product Management Team

Overview
This communication plan outlines how the Product team will keep internal stakeholders informed about product strategy, roadmap progress, feature launches, and key decisions during Q3 2026. A well-executed stakeholder communication plan builds organizational alignment, reduces duplicate questions, and enables cross-functional partners to plan effectively.

Stakeholder Communication Channels and Cadence

Weekly Product Update (Every Monday)
Audience: Product team, Engineering, and direct management chain. Format: Async written update distributed via Slack in the #product-updates channel. Content: Launches completed in the prior week, launches planned for the coming week, blockers or decisions needed, key metric highlights from the prior week. Owner: Product Operations.

Monthly Product Review (First Thursday of Each Month)
Audience: Executive leadership team. Format: 60-minute in-person or video meeting. Content: Progress against OKRs, roadmap updates and changes, key customer insights, competitive intelligence highlights, and upcoming major milestones. Owner: VP of Product (David Okonkwo). Pre-read distributed 48 hours before the meeting.

Quarterly Roadmap Update (End of Each Quarter)
Audience: All AcmeTech employees. Format: Company all-hands presentation (30 minutes) followed by Q&A. Content: Product vision narrative, roadmap highlights for the upcoming quarter, reflection on the prior quarter's key launches and learnings, and feature preview for items coming later in the year. Owner: VP of Product. Accompanied by a written summary published in the internal wiki.

Sales and Customer Success Enablement Update (Bi-Weekly)
Audience: Sales, Customer Success, and Partnerships teams. Format: 30-minute video call with supporting enablement deck distributed in advance. Content: Upcoming feature launches relevant to customer conversations, competitive updates, and talking points for common customer questions about the roadmap. Owner: Product Marketing Manager in partnership with Product.

Customer Communication
AcmeTech communicates significant product updates to customers through the following channels: monthly product newsletter distributed to all organization administrators; in-app announcements for features that affect the user experience; and dedicated communications to affected customers for any changes that require action or impact existing workflows. All customer communications are reviewed by Marketing and Customer Success leadership before distribution.

Document ID: FIN-002
Category: Finance & Tax
Title: Corporate Credit Card Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Corporate Credit Card Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: Finance and Accounting

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions issues corporate credit cards to employees who regularly incur business expenses as part of their role. This policy governs the issuance, use, and management of corporate credit cards to ensure proper financial controls, accurate expense tracking, and compliance with AcmeTech's budgetary standards.

Card Issuance Eligibility
Corporate credit cards are issued to employees at the Senior Manager level and above, employees in roles that require frequent business travel, and other employees as approved by the CFO based on documented business need. New employees in eligible roles must complete a minimum of 90 days of service before a card is issued. Card requests must be submitted by the employee's manager to the Finance team with a written business justification.

Authorized Use
Corporate credit cards are to be used solely for legitimate AcmeTech business expenses. Authorized categories include business travel, client meals and entertainment with prior manager approval, approved procurement purchases, and other pre-approved business expenditures. Under no circumstances may corporate cards be used for personal purchases, cash advances, or any expenditure outside the scope of approved business activities.

Expense Reconciliation
Cardholders are required to reconcile all corporate card transactions within 10 business days of each month-end statement date. Reconciliation must be completed through the expense management system, with receipts attached for each transaction. Transactions that cannot be reconciled with a valid business justification will be charged back to the cardholder through payroll deduction.

Reporting Lost or Stolen Cards
Cardholders must immediately report a lost or stolen corporate card to the Finance team and to the card issuer's 24-hour hotline. AcmeTech will not be responsible for unauthorized charges incurred after the card was reported missing. The Finance team will issue a replacement card within five business days of the report.

Card Suspension and Termination
Corporate cards will be immediately suspended upon an employee's termination of employment. Managers are responsible for ensuring cards are deactivated for employees who transfer to roles where card usage is no longer required. Repeated policy violations, including personal use of the card, failure to reconcile, or late submissions, will result in card revocation and potential disciplinary action.

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions recognizes that flexible workplace arrangements can enhance employee productivity, job satisfaction, and work-life balance. This Work From Home Policy governs the conditions under which employees may work remotely and establishes the standards of professional conduct, availability, and data security expected of remote employees.

Eligibility
Work from home arrangements are available to employees who have completed their probationary period and whose role has been assessed as suitable for remote work by their manager and HR Business Partner. Roles requiring on-site physical presence, client-facing duties in person, or access to secure facilities are not eligible for regular work from home arrangements. Eligibility may be reviewed and adjusted at any time based on business needs.

Standard Remote Work Arrangements
AcmeTech employees on approved work from home arrangements may work remotely up to three days per week, with a minimum of two days per week spent in the designated office location. Employees wishing to work from home on specific days must inform their manager by 9:00 AM on the day in question and ensure availability via AcmeTech's standard communication tools throughout core hours of 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM local time.

Home Office Requirements
Employees working from home are responsible for maintaining a safe, ergonomic, and distraction-free work environment. AcmeTech will provide a remote work equipment stipend of up to $500 per year to support home office setup. Employees must use AcmeTech-approved devices and must connect to the company VPN at all times when accessing internal systems remotely. Use of personal devices to access company data requires prior approval from the IT Security team.

Performance and Availability Standards
Remote employees are held to the same performance standards as office-based employees. Failure to meet performance expectations will result in the work from home arrangement being reviewed and potentially revoked. Employees on performance improvement plans are not eligible for work from home arrangements without explicit HR approval.

Compliance and Review
This policy will be reviewed annually by the People Operations team in consultation with department heads. Violations of this policy, including failure to maintain VPN connectivity or unapproved use of personal devices for company work, may result in revocation of remote work privileges and potential disciplinary action.

Document ID: HR-014
Category: HR Policies
Title: Probationary Period Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Probationary Period Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: People Operations

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions uses a probationary period for all new employees to allow both the employee and the organization to assess fit and performance before confirming permanent employment. This policy outlines the duration, expectations, and outcomes of the probationary period.

Duration
The standard probationary period is 90 days from the employee's start date. For senior leadership roles at Director level and above, the probationary period is extended to 180 days. The probationary period may be extended for an additional 30 days by mutual agreement between the manager and HR if additional time is needed to assess performance.

Expectations During Probation
During the probationary period, new employees are expected to complete all required onboarding activities, demonstrate the competencies and behaviors required for their role, and meet any specific performance milestones established in their onboarding plan. Managers are expected to provide structured feedback at 30, 60, and 90 days through formal check-in meetings documented in the HR system.

Leave During Probation
Employees in their probationary period accrue annual leave but may not take leave without specific HR approval. Sick leave may be taken as required. Any absence of five or more consecutive days may result in the probationary period being extended by the length of the absence.

Completion of Probation
Upon successful completion of the probationary period, the employee's status is confirmed as permanent. The manager will communicate this formally in a written confirmation letter sent through the HR portal. Salary reviews associated with successful completion will be processed in the next available payroll cycle.

Termination During Probation
Employment may be terminated during the probationary period with one week's written notice if performance or conduct does not meet expectations. Termination during probation does not require progression through the standard disciplinary process, though HR must be consulted before any termination decision is made.

Document ID: HR-001
Category: HR Policies
Title: Annual Leave Policy

AcmeTech Solutions Annual Leave Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: People Operations

Policy Statement
AcmeTech Solutions is committed to providing employees with adequate time away from work to rest, recharge, and attend to personal matters. This Annual Leave Policy outlines employee entitlements, accrual methods, approval procedures, and compliance expectations applicable to all full-time and part-time employees.

Leave Entitlements
All full-time employees are entitled to a minimum of 20 days of paid annual leave per calendar year, accruing at a rate of 1.67 days per month. Employees with more than five years of continuous service receive an additional five days, bringing total entitlement to 25 days. Part-time employees receive leave on a pro-rated basis proportional to their contracted hours. Employees in their probationary period may accrue leave but may not take leave until the probationary period is successfully completed, unless approved by the HR department under exceptional circumstances.

Carryover Rules
A maximum of five unused annual leave days may be carried over from one calendar year to the next. Any accrued leave in excess of five days at year-end will be forfeited unless an employee has an active approved leave request that could not be fulfilled due to business requirements. Employees must request carryover extensions in writing to their HR Business Partner no later than November 30.

Leave Request and Approval Process
Employees must submit leave requests through the AcmeTech HR self-service portal with a minimum of five business days notice for absences of one to three days, and a minimum of ten business days notice for absences of four or more consecutive days. Requests are subject to manager approval, which should be granted or declined within three business days of submission. Managers are responsible for ensuring adequate team coverage during leave periods. Denial of a leave request must be accompanied by a written explanation and a proposed alternative leave window.

Leave During Notice Period
Employees who have resigned or been issued notice of termination may take approved annual leave during the notice period at the manager's discretion. In cases where leave cannot be accommodated, accrued leave balances will be paid out in the final paycheck in accordance with applicable employment law.

Policy Compliance
Failure to obtain leave approval before absence, falsification of leave records, or abuse of the leave policy may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination. Employees with concerns about leave entitlements or approvals should contact the HR department at hrteam@acmetechsolutions.com.

Category: Administration

Overview
This article guides organization administrators through the steps to successfully onboard new team members to AcmeTech, from initial account invitation through first-day setup and training resources.

Step 1: Invite the New User
Navigate to Account Settings > Users and click "Invite User." Enter the new team member's work email address and select the appropriate role for their responsibilities. If your organization uses SSO, the new user will be provisioned automatically when they first sign in using your corporate identity provider; a manual invitation may not be required depending on your SSO configuration. If you are using AcmeTech's native authentication, the invitation email will be sent from noreply@acmetechsolutions.com. The invitation expires after 72 hours; you can resend it from the Users section if needed.

Step 2: Assign Groups and Permissions
If your organization uses custom groups to manage access to specific projects, customers, or features, add the new team member to the appropriate groups before their first login. Navigate to Account Settings > Groups, select the relevant group, and add the new user. Group membership determines which data and features the user can access within their assigned role.

Step 3: Share Getting Started Resources
AcmeTech provides a Getting Started guide and video tutorial series accessible from the Help Center at help.acmetechsolutions.com. Share the following resources with new team members: the Getting Started Guide for their specific role (Member, Admin, or View Only), the AcmeTech Quick Start video (15 minutes), and the keyboard shortcuts reference card. The in-app onboarding checklist will appear automatically for new users on their first login and guides them through the platform's core features.

Step 4: Schedule a Walkthrough
For teams onboarding five or more new users at once, AcmeTech offers a complimentary onboarding webinar facilitated by a Customer Success team member. Professional and Enterprise plan customers can schedule an onboarding session by contacting their Customer Success Manager or emailing onboarding@acmetechsolutions.com. The session covers platform navigation, key workflows, and best practices relevant to your team's use case.

Step 5: Monitor Adoption
After onboarding, track new user adoption through the User Activity Report in Account Settings > Users > Reports. Users who have not completed the in-app onboarding checklist or who have not logged in within seven days of invitation can be identified in this report. Follow up with these users to address any barriers to adoption. AcmeTech's Customer Success team can provide additional enablement resources on request.

Title: User Management Guide

Article: Managing Users in Your AcmeTech Organization
Category: Administration
Last Updated: April 2026

Overview
This article describes how organization administrators can add, manage, and remove users within their AcmeTech account. Proper user management ensures that the right people have appropriate access to your organization's data and features while maintaining security and compliance.

Adding New Users
Organization administrators can invite new users by navigating to Account Settings > Users and clicking "Invite User." Enter the new user's email address and select their role. AcmeTech provides three default roles: Member (standard user access to all features assigned to their account), Admin (ability to manage users, billing, and organization settings), and View Only (read-only access to all organization data). Click "Send Invitation." The invited user will receive an email invitation from noreply@acmetechsolutions.com with a link to create their account. Invitation links expire after 72 hours; expired invitations can be resent from the Users section.

Managing User Roles and Permissions
User roles can be updated at any time by navigating to Account Settings > Users, locating the user, and selecting "Edit Role." Role changes take effect immediately. Custom roles with granular permissions are available on the Enterprise plan and can be configured in Account Settings > Users > Roles. Changes to user roles are recorded in the organization's audit log.

Removing Users
To remove a user from your organization, navigate to Account Settings > Users, locate the user, and click "Remove User." Confirm the removal in the dialog that appears. Removed users immediately lose access to all AcmeTech organization resources. Their user account is deactivated but their user data, including any records they created, remains associated with your organization. If SSO is enabled, removing a user from AcmeTech does not automatically remove them from your identity provider; you must deactivate the user in both systems.

Bulk User Management
Organizations managing more than 50 users can use the CSV import feature to add or update users in bulk. Download the import template from Account Settings > Users > Import Users, populate it with the required user fields, and upload the completed file. The import process validates all entries and reports any errors before making changes. Bulk user removal is also supported through the CSV import with a "remove" action column.

User Activity and Access Reviews
Administrators are encouraged to conduct periodic user access reviews to ensure that only active employees retain AcmeTech access. The User Activity Report, available in Account Settings > Users > Reports, shows each user's last login date and activity summary. Users who have not logged in for more than 90 days are flagged in the report. AcmeTech also supports automated deprovisioning via SCIM for organizations that require lifecycle management integrated with their HR system.

Document ID: ENG-007
Category: Engineering Documentation
Title: Authentication Architecture

AcmeTech Solutions Authentication Architecture
Version: 2.0
Last Updated: January 2026
Owner: Security Engineering Team

Overview
This document describes the authentication architecture for AcmeTech's platform, covering both customer-facing authentication and internal service-to-service authentication. A robust, layered authentication architecture is foundational to the security of AcmeTech's systems and customer data.

Customer Authentication
AcmeTech customers authenticate to the platform using OAuth 2.0 with the Authorization Code Flow with PKCE for web and mobile applications. Upon successful authentication, the identity provider issues a short-lived JSON Web Token (JWT) with a 15-minute expiration and a refresh token with a 30-day expiration. JWTs are signed using RS256 and validated by the API Gateway before any request is forwarded to backend services. Token rotation is enforced on every refresh token use.

Identity Provider
AcmeTech operates its own identity provider built on the open-source Keycloak platform, customized for our multi-tenant architecture. Each customer organization is provisioned as a separate Keycloak realm, enabling complete logical isolation of authentication contexts. The identity provider supports username/password authentication, SSO via SAML 2.0 and OIDC federation with corporate identity providers, and TOTP-based multi-factor authentication.

Multi-Factor Authentication
MFA is required for all AcmeTech administrative console users and is strongly recommended for all end users. MFA enforcement at the customer organization level can be configured by the organization's administrator. AcmeTech supports TOTP-based authenticators and WebAuthn hardware security keys. SMS-based MFA is not supported due to known vulnerabilities associated with SIM swapping attacks.

Service-to-Service Authentication
Internal microservice-to-microservice communication is authenticated using mutual TLS (mTLS). Each service is issued a short-lived X.509 certificate by the cluster certificate authority. Certificate rotation occurs every 24 hours and is managed automatically by the service mesh. Services validate peer certificates against the trusted CA bundle at connection establishment. Service accounts in Kubernetes are issued projected service account tokens with audience-restricted claims for additional authorization enforcement.

Session Management
Customer sessions are managed through the identity provider with activity-based session timeout after 60 minutes of inactivity and an absolute maximum session duration of 12 hours. Session revocation is propagated to the API Gateway token validation cache within 30 seconds of logout or administrative revocation. The authentication audit log captures all authentication events, including successful logins, failed attempts, MFA challenges, and session revocations.

AcmeTech Solutions Employee Travel Policy
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Policy Owner: People Operations and Finance

Policy Statement
This Travel Policy establishes guidelines for all business-related travel undertaken by AcmeTech Solutions employees. The policy is designed to ensure that travel is necessary, cost-effective, and conducted in compliance with company financial controls. All employees who travel for business purposes are required to follow this policy in full.

Pre-Travel Approval
All domestic travel must be approved by the employee's direct manager prior to booking. International travel requires approval from both the direct manager and the relevant department head. Employees must submit a travel request through the AcmeTech expense management system, including purpose of travel, estimated costs, and travel dates, a minimum of five business days before the intended departure date for domestic trips and ten business days for international trips.

Booking and Class of Travel
All airfare must be booked through AcmeTech's preferred travel management platform. Economy class is the standard for all flights of less than six hours in duration. Business class may be approved for flights exceeding six hours on a case-by-case basis with VP-level approval. Hotel accommodations must be selected from AcmeTech's preferred hotel list or comparable options within the company's published nightly rate limits: $200 per night for domestic travel and $250 per night for international travel.

Ground Transportation and Meals
Employees should use ride-sharing services or public transportation where practical. Rental car usage must be pre-approved and restricted to trips where other transportation options are not viable. A daily meal allowance of $60 domestic and $85 international is provided. Alcohol expenses are not reimbursable under this policy.

Expense Reimbursement
All travel expenses must be submitted through the expense management system within 15 business days of returning from travel. Receipts are required for all individual expenses over $25. Expenses submitted without receipts will not be reimbursed. The Finance team will process approved reimbursements within 10 business days.

Non-Reimbursable Expenses
Personal entertainment, upgrades, seat selections beyond standard economy, travel insurance purchased without prior approval, and expenses incurred for personal guests are not reimbursable. Employees who incur non-reimbursable expenses will be personally responsible for those costs.

AcmeTech Solutions Product Roadmap
FY2026 – FY2027
Version: 3.1

Executive Summary
This roadmap outlines AcmeTech's product strategy and planned feature investments for the next 18 months. The roadmap is organized by strategic theme and reflects the product team's translation of company OKRs, user research findings, and competitive analysis into a prioritized set of product initiatives. The roadmap is reviewed and refreshed quarterly in alignment with the OKR planning cycle.

Strategic Themes
AcmeTech's product roadmap is organized around three strategic themes that reflect our North Star vision of becoming the operating system for enterprise knowledge work. Theme 1 – Enterprise Readiness: Investments in security, compliance, user management, and administrative controls that enable AcmeTech to serve the needs of large enterprise customers at scale. Theme 2 – Intelligence and Automation: Investments in AI-powered features that help users work faster and surface insights from their data without manual effort. Theme 3 – Platform Extensibility: Investments in the API platform, integration ecosystem, and developer tools that enable customers and partners to build on top of AcmeTech.

FY2026 H1 Initiatives (Q1-Q2)
Enterprise Readiness: Advanced role-based access control with field-level permissions, SOC 2 Type II audit completion and report distribution, SCIM-based user provisioning for enterprise identity providers. Intelligence and Automation: AI-powered document summarization and smart search, automated workflow triggers based on data change events, intelligent anomaly detection for key business metrics. Platform Extensibility: Public API v3 launch with expanded endpoints and improved developer documentation, native Salesforce and HubSpot integrations, webhook improvements with enhanced delivery reliability.

FY2026 H2 Initiatives (Q3-Q4)
Enterprise Readiness: Data residency controls for EU and APAC regions, advanced audit log analytics, enterprise reporting suite with scheduled report delivery. Intelligence and Automation: Predictive analytics module for trend forecasting, AI-assisted data quality recommendations, natural language query interface for analytics. Platform Extensibility: Partner integration marketplace launch, mobile SDK for iOS and Android, expanded Zapier and Make integration catalog.

FY2027 Horizon Planning
Items on the FY2027 horizon include real-time collaboration features, an industry-specific vertical product module for financial services, expanded localization for Japanese and Korean markets, and a self-hosted deployment option for customers with strict data sovereignty requirements. Document ID: PM-002
Category: Product Management
Title: OKR Planning Framework

AcmeTech Solutions Product OKR Planning Framework
Cycle: FY2026
Owner: Product Management Team

Overview
AcmeTech uses Objectives and Key Results (OKRs) as its primary goal-setting framework for aligning the product organization around measurable outcomes. This document describes the OKR planning process, the structure of well-formed objectives and key results, and the cadence for tracking and reviewing OKR progress.

OKR Structure
An OKR consists of one qualitative Objective that describes a meaningful, ambitious goal, paired with two to five quantitative Key Results that measure whether the objective has been achieved. Objectives should be inspiring and directional. Key Results must be specific, measurable, and time-bound. Key Results are not activity metrics (e.g., "complete the feature") but outcome metrics (e.g., "increase feature adoption to 65% of active users within 90 days of launch").

Planning Cadence
AcmeTech's OKR cycle is annual at the company level and quarterly at the team level. Company-level OKRs are set by the executive leadership team in January for the full fiscal year. Product team-level OKRs are set quarterly, aligned to and in support of the company-level objectives. Individual contributors set their personal OKRs in alignment with their team's quarterly OKRs. OKR drafts are reviewed in a two-week alignment period before being finalized.

FY2026 Company-Level Product OKRs
Objective 1: Become the enterprise knowledge platform of choice. Key Results: Achieve a net revenue retention rate of 115%; grow enterprise customer count (ACV > $100K) by 40%; achieve a G2 crowd rating of 4.7 or higher by Q4 2026.

Objective 2: Deliver AI-powered experiences that create measurable user value. Key Results: Launch and achieve 50% adoption of the AI document summarization feature among weekly active users by Q3; reduce average time-on-task for the three most common workflows by 30% as measured by in-product analytics; achieve an NPS of 60 or higher among users of AI-powered features.

Objective 3: Build a platform that attracts a thriving developer and partner ecosystem. Key Results: Achieve 500 registered developers in the AcmeTech developer program by Q4; launch 10 certified partner integrations in the integration marketplace; achieve a developer documentation satisfaction score of 4.5 or higher.

Scoring and Review
OKR scores are assessed at the end of each quarter using a 0.0 to 1.0 scale. A score of 0.7 is considered a successful outcome; consistently scoring 1.0 may indicate the objective was not sufficiently ambitious. OKR retrospectives are conducted at the end of each quarter to assess performance, identify blockers, and inform the next cycle's planning. Results are shared transparently across the product organization.

Document ID: PM-007
Category: Product Management
Title: Product Launch Checklist

AcmeTech Solutions Product Launch Checklist
Version: 2.0
Owner: Product Management Team

Overview
This checklist ensures that all required activities are completed before a product feature or release is launched to customers. The checklist is organized into four phases: Pre-Launch, Launch Day, Post-Launch, and Retrospective. The product manager is responsible for coordinating completion of the checklist with all relevant stakeholders.

Phase 1: Pre-Launch (T-4 Weeks to T-1 Week)

Product Readiness
- Feature is complete, tested, and accepted by the product manager against defined acceptance criteria.
- All identified bugs are resolved or have documented rationale for deferral.
- Feature flag is configured and tested in staging and production environments.
- Experiment design is documented if launching as an A/B test.

Documentation and Support Readiness
- Help Center article is written, reviewed, and published in draft status.
- Customer Support team has been briefed with a dedicated enablement session.
- Customer Support FAQ is prepared for anticipated customer questions.
- In-app tooltips and onboarding guidance for the feature are implemented.

Go-to-Market Readiness
- Sales and Customer Success enablement materials are ready, including feature overview, demo script, and anticipated objections.
- Marketing launch content is drafted and reviewed: blog post, email, in-app notification, social posts.
- Customer communication plan is approved by Marketing and Customer Success leadership.
- Press or analyst communication is coordinated if the launch merits external visibility.

Phase 2: Launch Day

- Feature flag enabled for target user segment per launch plan.
- Help Center article published from draft to live.
- In-app launch notifications activated.
- Customer email launched per the communication plan.
- Monitoring dashboards reviewed for anomalies 30 minutes post-launch.
- Slack launch announcement posted to #product-launches channel.

Phase 3: Post-Launch (T+1 Week and T+30 Days)

- T+1 Week: Initial metrics reviewed against experiment or launch success criteria. Customer Support ticket volume related to the feature reviewed.
- T+30 Days: Full metric analysis conducted. Ship/iterate/revert decision documented.

Phase 4: Retrospective (T+30 Days)

- Launch retrospective meeting conducted with Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Support.
- Retrospective findings documented and shared in the internal knowledge base.

Document ID: HR-007
